IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify

Helps verify that all resource fix-ups have been applied appropriately.

DWORD Verify(
  BOOL StampResources
);

Parameters

StampResources
[in] When StampResources is TRUE, this function will parse the push-buffer and replace all physical addresses referenced in the push-buffer with invalid values that would cause a GPU error if executed. When StampResources is FALSE, this function parses the push-buffer and verifies that no invalid physical addresses remain (an invalid physical address would imply that the caller missed doing a resource fix-up). When such an error is detected, Direct3D outputs the approximate offset to the debugger.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is S_OK.

Known Issues

The following is a known issue with IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify:

  • After IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Jump is used in a push-buffer fixup, calling Verify on that push-buffer will result in the assertion failure "ERR[D3D]: Assertion failure: instruction != 1".

Remarks

This is a debug-only function for help in verifying that all resource fix-ups have been applied appropriately. Forgetting or missing a fix-up in a push-buffer can cause random, hard-to-track-down errors, unless this function is used.

Because the CPU has to read from write-combined memory for the non-D3DPUSHBUFFER_RUN_USING_CPU_COPY cases, this function can be very slow. Because any push-buffer that has had IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify (TRUE) run on it will cause the GPU to automatically generate an interrupt and break into the debugger if there is a missed fix-up, for performance reasons the caller might want to start calling IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify (FALSE) only when tracking down the reasons for such GPU generated errors.

The intended use of this method is as a debugging aid when recording push-buffers that are saved to disk and loaded later (such as when static push-buffers are created at author time). The caller would call IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify (TRUE) after recording the push-buffer and just before saving to disk. Then at run time, if the GPU generates a weird error interrupt while running the push-buffer, do IDirect3DPushBuffer8::Verify (FALSE) before it's run again to find any missing fix-ups.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

Push-Buffer Overview
